title: Racking board and diving board assemblies and methods
abstract: A drilling rig assembly and methods of assembling the rig are described. The drilling rig assembly can include a mast, a traveling assembly coupled to the mast, and a racking board configured to be removably coupled to the traveling assembly and removably coupled to the mast. The racking board can have a first end and a first plurality of fasteners located at a first end of the racking board and a racking frame. A diving board can be configured to be removably coupled to the racking board. The diving board can include a second plurality of fasteners, each adapted to removably couple with a corresponding one of the first plurality of fasteners of the racking board.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A drilling rig assembly, comprising: a mast; a traveling assembly coupled to the mast; and a racking board configured to be removably coupled to the traveling assembly and removably coupled to the mast. 2. The drilling rig assembly of claim 1, wherein: the traveling assembly includes a dolly movably coupled to the mast; and the racking board is configured to be removably coupled to the dolly. 3. The drilling rig assembly of claim 2, wherein the dolly comprises a pair of movable arms coupled to the mast. 4. The drilling rig assembly of claim 2, wherein the dolly is coupled to a top drive and configured to be lifted up and down a length of the mast. 5. The drilling rig assembly of claim 3, wherein the dolly comprises a cross beam extending between the pair of arms. 6. The drilling rig assembly of claim 1, further comprising a control system for controlling movement of the travelling assembly. 7. The drilling rig assembly of claim 8, wherein the control system is configured to automatically cease movement of the travelling assembly when the racking board has reached a specified position. 8. The drilling rig assembly of claim 1, further comprising: a diving board configured to removably coupled to the racking board. 9. The drilling rig assembly of claim 8, wherein the racking board comprises a first plurality of fasteners located at a first end of the racking board and a racking frame, and the diving board comprises a second plurality of fasteners, each adapted to removably couple with a corresponding one of the first plurality of fasteners of the racking board. 10. The drilling rig assembly of claim 9, wherein the diving board comprises a first end and a second end, wherein the first end is attached to the racking board and a second end is attached to the mast. 11. The drilling rig assembly according to claim 9, wherein the racking frame of the racking board defines a first surface proximal the first end of the racking board and wherein the first end of the diving board defines a second surface adapted to rest against the first surface of the racking frame. 12. A method of assembling a drilling rig assembly, comprising: raising a mast; removably coupling a racking board to a traveling assembly proximate a floor of a drilling rig, wherein the traveling assembly is movably coupled to the raised mast; aligning the racking board with the travelling assembly; raising the racking board to a first height along the raised mast using the traveling assembly; removably coupling the racking board to the raised mast at the first height; and decoupling the racking board from the travelling assembly. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein a control system controls movement of the travelling assembly. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the control system comprises a computer system programmed to automatically stop movement of the racking board once it has reached the first height. 15. The method of claim 12, wherein the travelling assembly comprises a dolly configured to movably couple to the mast and removably couple to the racking board. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the raising of the racking board comprises: removably pinning the racking board to the dolly; raising the dolly to the first height; securing the racking board to the mast at the first height; and decoupling the racking board from the dolly. 17. The method of claim 12, wherein the racking board is removably coupled to the raised mast with one or more fastener systems. 18. The method of claim 12, further comprising: adjusting the raised racking board to a second height via the travelling assembly. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the adjusting of the raised racking board comprises: coupling the raised racking board to the travelling assembly; and decoupling the raised racking board from the mast. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the adjusting of the raised racking board further comprises: moving the raised racking board from the first height to the second height; coupling the raised racking board to the mast at the second height; and decoupling the raised racking board from the travelling assembly. 21. The method of claim 12, further comprising: replacing the raised racking board. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein the replacing comprises: lowering the raised racking board with the travelling assembly; decoupling the lowered racking board from the travelling assembly; 

 coupling a new racking board to the travelling assembly; and raising the new racking board to the first height. 23. The method of claim 15, further comprising: providing a detachable diving board; raising the diving board to the first height along the mast; and removably coupling the diving board to the racking board. 24. The method of claim 23, further comprising: aligning the diving board with the traveling assembly of the rig, with one or more rails of the mast, or with a dolly removably coupled to the traveling assembly; removably coupling the diving board to the traveling assembly or the dolly; raising, via the traveling assembly or the dolly, the diving board to the first height, wherein the diving board is located in a middle portion of the racking board in the first height; and upon coupled with the racking board, decoupling the diving board from the traveling assembly or the dolly. 25. The method of claim 23, further comprising: decoupling the diving board from the racking board; coupling the diving board to the traveling assembly or the dolly lowering the diving board via the travelling assembly or the dolly to the rig floor; and decoupling the diving board from the travelling assembly or the dolly.
